explanations.1: This line tells the spell-checker to ignore certain technical terms specific to Oracle database tools so it doesn't mark them as misspellings.
explanations.3: This starts with a pre-built PHP image that includes PHP and a FastCGI process manager, so you don't have to install them from scratch.
explanations.5: The && chains multiple commands together, and --no-install-recommends installs only what's needed without extra optional packages, keeping the image smaller.
explanations.11: This deletes temporary package lists after installation to reduce the Docker image size, since those files aren't needed anymore.
explanations.18: ARG defines a build-time variable that you can pass when building the Docker image; it's not available when the container runs.
explanations.27: This creates a symbolic link (shortcut) pointing to the versioned instantclient folder with a generic name, so code can always reference the same path regardless of version.
explanations.28: sh -c runs a shell command that writes the Oracle library path to a system configuration file, telling the system where to find Oracle libraries.
explanations.29: ldconfig reads the configuration file from the previous line and updates the system's library cache so the operating system can find the Oracle libraries.
explanations.32: This pipes (|) a configuration string directly into pecl install, telling it where the Oracle client library is located while installing the oci8 PHP extension.
